project_title: Discovery Engine - eight-stage cross-disciplinary research-question discovery apparatus
runtime_target: local-first web or desktop application with a graph-backed research-stage dependency model
operational_end_state: A researcher can start with a seed question, discover transferable mechanisms from other fields, pressure-test analogies, move the result through academic research stages, and see downstream sections marked stale when upstream claims, methods, or evidence change.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of Nexus Research Pipeline: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
required_modules_to_build: Observatory intake and problem decomposition | Scanner and source-field discovery | Bridge mechanism-transfer mapper | Cross-Pollination and XPol fit-pressure tester | Incubator research-object builder | Forge writing/export gate | stage-dependency stale-state engine
source_status: 5 source-application rows; 0 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 5 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output | dependency test: upstream change marks downstream work stale
acceptance_tests: Open or run the application slice; feed the fixture; verify output artifact, state persistence or export, error boundary, and source-governed acceptance condition.
promotion_stage: prototype-to-full-build; source and capture checks still govern release language
current_status: file-backed proof build; needs live reviewer capture for stronger public demonstration
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `row7`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.
first_full_build: Implement a live seed-question walkthrough with one cross-field transfer and one downstream stale-state propagation.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
